REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (839 Sanchez St, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
Off Calendar Aug-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Daniel Cunningham (Petitioner/Trustee).

The sale was confirmed at $2,150,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed June 29, 2021. The Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property is under submission subject to receipt and review of the order. An additional bond in the amount of $2,184,000.00 is required. The Court extends time to close on the sale to 30-days.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (839 Sanchez St, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
Off Calendar Jul-13-2021 Continued To Aug-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi, via ZOOM, counsel for Daniel Cunningham, via ZOOM, Conservator; Deborah Fox, via ZOOM, court appointed counsel for Donald Wilson, via ZOOM, Conservatee.

At the outset, the court queries Mr. Wilson if he objects to the sale of the property located on Sanchez Street. Mr. Wilson confirms. The court then queries Mr. Wilson regarding how things are going at the facility where he is currently residing. Mr. Wilson reports that he is not comfortable at the current residence and would like to return home. The court then queries Mr. Wilson regarding the incidents at his home which led to his current placement. Mr. Wilson reports that he vaguely recalls the incidents in question. The court then turns to the possible sale of the property specifically waiving publication. Ms. Alavi reports that they would like to sell the home sooner rather than later and that a revised order would be submitted which would include language regarding a request to waive publication. The court then turns to Ms. Fox who reports that although Mr. Wilson is upset at the loss of autonomy, he does understand that currently the best option for him is to sell the Sanchez property in order to downsize to a smaller property. She also reports Mr. Wilson's desire to move to a better location and for the removal of Mr. Cunningham as Conservator.

After additional discussion, Accounting Petition #1 is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Continued To May-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: [Heard with PCN-17-300534]

Appearances: Avid Alavi, Esq., counsel for Daniel Cunningham, former Conservator (appearing by video conference); Scott Poling, Esq., counsel for Minna Fernan, Successor Conservator of the Estate (appearing by video conference).

Court and counsel review the unauthorized IRA distribution and the comingling of funds from the conservatorship and the trust. Ms. Alavi notes that the trust was established by substituted judgment and was initially funded by funds from the conservatorship estate. Nonetheless, separate accountings are required.

The Court inquires as to when Mr. Cunningham would be resigning as trustee which gives rise to a discussion as to the sale of the real property. After discussion, counsel agree that Mr. Cunningham should stay on as Trustee as that would facilitate the sale of the real property. Counsel also agree that sale of the real property is appropriate as the Conservatee's financial situation is dire and that the Conservatee would not be able to reside in the property in any event as it is not economically feasible.

The Court notes that the information provided to support the sale of the real property is stale and declines to approve the sale without more current data. The Successor Conservator should also opine as to the propriety of the sale. The Court is inclined to allow - but not approve - the accounting but will take the matter under submission. The Court also indicates that it may be appropriate to appoint an attorney to represent the interests of the Conservatee.

The Court continues the matter to May 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Ms. Alavi is to submit a supplemental declaration updating the accounting and the information to support the sale of the real property.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
